Smurfs: The Lost Village (2017)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $45,020,282Details
International Box Office $152,558,304
Worldwide Box Office $197,578,586
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $5,904,030
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $3,562,381
Total $9,466,411
Further financial details...

Synopsis

A mysterious map sets Smurfette and her best friends Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty on an exciting and thrilling race through the Forbidden Forest filled with magical creatures to find a mysterious lost village before the evil wizard Gargamel does. Embarking on a rollercoaster journey full of action and danger, the Smurfs are on a course that leads to the discovery of the biggest secret in Smurf history!

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$13,210,449 (29.3% of total gross)
Legs:3.41 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:22.8% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$60,000,000 (worldwide box office is 3.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,610 opening theaters/3,610 max. theaters, 5.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $60,027,049

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 7th, 2017 (Wide) by Sony Pictures
International Releases: March 24th, 2017 (Special Engagement), released as Smurfai: pamirštas kaimelis (Lithuania)
March 24th, 2017 (Wide) (Russia (CIS))
March 29th, 2017 (Wide) (Indonesia)
March 29th, 2017 (Wide) (Belgium)
March 30th, 2017 (Wide) (Iraq)
Video Release: June 20th, 2017 by Columbia Pictures Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG for some mild action and rude humor
(Rating bulletin 2460 (Cert #50628), 1/25/2017)
Running Time: 90 minutes
Franchise: The Smurfs
Cast, crew, or production detail: Animal Lead, Directing Yourself, Prologue, Reboot, Scene in End Credits, Voiceover/Narration
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Shot in 3-D
Geographical setting: Underground
Plot point: Non-Chronological, Rescue, Road Trip
Subgenre: Family Adventure
Target audience: Family Movie
Source:Based on Comic/Graphic Novel
Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Digital Animation
Creative Type:Kids Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Kerner Entertainment Company, Columbia Pictures, LStar Capital, Wanda Media, Sony Pictures Animation
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

A mysterious map sets Smurfette and her best friends Brainy, Clumsy and Hefty on an exciting and thrilling race through the Forbidden Forest filled with magical creatures to find a mysterious lost village before the evil wizard Gargamel does. Embarking on a rollercoaster journey full of action and danger, the Smurfs are on a course that leads to the discovery of the biggest secret in Smurf history!
